## Key Themes

1. **Parse product overview** — Turn any website into a structured API; variable pricing challenges with bandwidth and anti-bot costs; shifting from monthly to usage-based pricing
2. **Zo × Parse integration hypothesis** — Parse as a natural extension of Zo's data ingestion layer; non-technical users could leverage Parse APIs within Zo workflows
3. **Non-technical ICP alignment** — Both products serve users who lack engineering backgrounds but want software capabilities; garbage collection industry example as emblematic use case
4. **V's Zo relationship and ambassador role** — V positioned as power user / brand advocate; building open-source starter repo for Zo; intro pipeline to Ben and Rob (Zo founders)
5. **Warm intro to Zo founders** — Alex consented to introduction; V to connect him with Ben and Rob; Alex also interested in joining Zo Discord (~1,000 members)

## Follow-Up Commitments

- V to intro Alex to Ben and Rob (Zo founders)
- V to send Parse demos with Zo use cases (1-2 week timeline)
- V to provide product feedback on Parse's latest update
- Alex to try Zo product and explore Discord community

## Discovery Signals

- **Usage-based pricing alignment:** Both Zo and Parse converging on usage-based models — natural billing compatibility
- **Developer ecosystem gap:** Parse sees demand from industries with zero API infrastructure — aligns with Zo's thesis of empowering non-technical builders
- **Community distribution channel:** Zo Discord (~1K members) as organic distribution for Parse
- **Integration packaging opportunity:** V's starter repo could bundle Parse integration as a default capability
